programs.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programs.desc: The host shows creatures that amaze scientists.It presents interesting facts and explains where myths about certain animals come from.

programs.title: Life Below Zero: The Thaw
programs.desc: Alaskans are preparing for a thaw.Snow-covered peaks turn into obstacle courses.Hikers encounter rushing streams and hungry predators and swarms of insects.

programs.title: Once Upon A Time In Space
Planet Earth III
programs.desc: The third part of the nature documentary, produced over almost five years, concludes the trilogy almost two decades after the first series was broadcast.Its creators used pioneering film technologies, including:light drones, super-fast cameras and remotely controlled deep-sea submarines.In eight episodes, viewers will visit the most remote jungles, the darkest ocean depths, the world's largest caves and the hottest deserts and steppes.They will learn the story about nature's will to survive and the adaptive abilities of various species.The Earth is at a tipping point and changes in the natural world are felt in every ecosystem and affect numerous creatures.The series highlights the growing need to protect and restore nature.In the last episode, viewers will meet inspiring people from various corners of the world who are the face of change and saving the Earth.They are committed to risking their lives to protect the planet.Sir David Attenborough will guide the series.

programs.title: Life Below Zero
programs.desc: Daily struggles of daredevils from various regions of Alaska.The inhabitants of the four houses face further hardships of life in a frosty climate and fight to survive the long and dangerous winter.
